summary: Import-time fetch and eval of unpinned remote JavaScript from unpkg.com
details: On require/import of the package (main entry src/hive.mjs) and on every CLI startup (bin entries hive, solve, task, review, configure-claude, start-screen, hive-screens, hive-telegram-bot), the package fetches https://unpkg.com/use-m/use.js with no version pin and passes the response body directly to eval(). The pattern `globalThis.use = (await eval(await (await fetch('https://unpkg.com/use-m/use.js')).text())).use;` appears at module top in src/config.lib.mjs:11, src/lib.mjs:35, src/hive.mjs, src/github.lib.mjs, src/claude.lib.mjs, src/telegram-bot.mjs, and ~40 other source files. There is no integrity check, no SRI, no version pin, and no fallback — every load of the package or its CLIs unconditionally executes whatever JavaScript unpkg.com serves at that path at that moment. A compromise of unpkg, the use-m maintainer account, or any intermediary on the network path between the installer's machine and unpkg yields arbitrary code execution on every machine that imports this package or runs its CLIs. The package additionally exposes a `--auto-cleanup` flag that runs `sudo rm -rf /tmp/* /var/tmp/*`; this is user-opt-in and not the basis for the recommendation but is worth operator awareness.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
